name: Café Jazz - July 2026
description: The32nd Editioncelebrates the unique musical relationship between theguitar and bass in jazz, highlighting their rich harmonic and melodic dialogue while exploring the expansive possibilities of improvisation.Started in 2022 by one of the pioneers of jazz in the city,Jagadeesh M R, Director ofThe Bangalore School of Musicand founder ofMoonArra World Fusion Ensemble, this is a monthly event, designed to building a community of jazz musicians in the city and foster a culture of creativity. It is a platform to connect with each other and audiences through jam sessions, lecture demonstrations and performances. The project has fostered a faithful community of musicians who participate and interact freely with younger jazz aspirants while audiences get a rare opportunity to watch, listen and understand the creative processes in jazz.Free entry for audiences.Musicians to register via Whatsapp +91 9844245577 for a spot.

name: The day my father became a bush...

location.name: Ranga Shankara
location.address: No. 36/2, 8th Cross Road, JP Nagar, 2nd Phase, Bangalore 560078, India

description: We are happy to presentThe Day my Father became a Bush... bytheater katinkaspringinsfeldat the 2026 edition ofRanga Shankara's AHA!Theatre for Children Festival! The piece byJoke van Leeuwenhas been adapted for stage by Susanne Koschig and Kathrin Blüchert.Toda is a courageous young girl fleeing from war across the border to her mother´s place, because her father back home gets conscripted and learns how to camouflage himself as a bush.Toda tells the story of her escape, her many different adventures along the way and the various people she met: money-grubbing peoplesmugglers, children who should give their toys away, a friendly commander who is unable to command.In a mixture of storytelling, acting, puppetry, shadow theatre and chalk drawing this solo performance looks at war and refugees from the perspective of a child. The audience sits on stage and is invited to become part of the story.TeamDirector:Susanne KoschigPerformer:Kathrin BlüchertTechnician:Neil GreigDate and timings:25.07.2026, 3:30 pm and 7:30 pmVenue:Ranga Shankara |Tickets:Box OfficeLanguage:English |Duration:50 min.Age:8+Kathrin Blüchert, also known astheatrekatinkaspringinsfeld, was born in Berlin. She initially worked in dance theatre and, from 1995 to 2003, in street theatre with the group “Los Dilettantos”, before training as a puppeteer at the Ernst Busch Academy of Dramatic Arts. She subsequently worked in various constellations, including at the TNBA in Bordeaux and, from 2012 to 2022, as a member of the ensemble at the Theater Waidspeicher in Erfurt.Since then, she has been working as a freelancer under the name “theater katinkaspringinsfeld”, based in Nuremberg, and tours with “Thalias Kompagnons” and “die exen”.After the Bangalore show,The Day my Father became a Bush... will travel toTrivandrumfor a show on July 28, 2026.The Day my Father became a Bush... is presented in India byGoethe-Institut / Max Mueller Bhavan BangaloreandGoethe-Zentrum Trivandrum.
